perturbation.original_text: Receiver shall use the same degree of care to protect and safeguard the confidentiality of Confidential Information as it uses to protect and safeguard the confidentiality of its own proprietary info rmation, and Receiver represents, warrants and covenants to VIDAR that such degree of care is reasonably designed to protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiver shall use a reasonable degree of care to protect the confidentiality of Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies the standard of care based on how the Receiver protects its own proprietary information. Removing the comparison to the receiver's own proprietary information and replacing it with 'a reasonable degree of care' introduces ambiguity. This creates a contradiction with Section 8, which asserts that VIDAR's confidential information is 'valuable' and that unauthorized use could cause 'irreparable harm'. A 'reasonable degree of care' may be insufficient to prevent such harm.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiver acknowledges and agrees that: (i) the Confidential Information is proprietary to and valuable information of VIDAR; (ii) VIDAR derives economic value from the Confidential Information not being generally known to other persons who can obtain economic value from its discl osure or use; (iii) any disclosure or unauthorized use of the Confidential Information could cause irreparable harm and loss to VIDAR; and (iv) if this Agr eement is breached, VIDAR can not be made whole by monetary damages. Accordingly, VIDAR, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled by law or in equity, shall be entitled to an injunction to prevent further breaches of this Agreem ent and to an order compelling specific performance of this Agreement. Receiver shall reimburse VIDAR for all costs and expenses, including attorney’s fees, incurr ed by VIDAR in enforcing the obligations of Receiver.
perturbation.justification: The original text established a standard of care based on the Receiver's own practices, including an assertion that the standard was reasonably designed to protect the Confidential Information. The changed text replaces this with a general 'reasonable degree of care'. The contradicted text emphasizes the 'valuable' nature of the information and the potential for 'irreparable harm' from unauthorized use. A 'reasonable degree of care' is a subjective standard, which may be insufficient, and thus directly contradicts the acknowledged need to protect valuable information that if breached, cannot be made whole by monetary damages. The original text creates more certainty and enforceability, but the changed text introduces ambiguity and conflicts with the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: All Intellectual Property that are origi nal works of authorship shall be considered works made for hire (as that phrase is used in Section 101 of the United States Copyright Act, 17 U.S.C. § 101 or analogous la w). If any Intellectual Property is found not to be a work made for hire and/or is owned by Receiver or any third party, Receiver hereby irrevocably (and shall cause any third party owner to) assigns and transfers all rights to and inte rests in such Intellectual Property to VIDAR without further consideration.
perturbation.changed_text: All Intellectual Property shall be considered works made for hire. If any Intellectual Property is found not to be a work made for hire and/or is owned by Receiver or any third party, Receiver hereby irrevocably (and shall cause any third party owner to) assigns and transfers all rights to and inte rests in such Intellectual Property to VIDAR without further consideration.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the reference to 'original works of authorship' and the specific citation of Section 101 of the United States Copyright Act creates an in-text contradiction. Section 5(a) defines Intellectual Property as arising 'from or relating to this Agreement'. By omitting 'original works of authorship', it implies that *any* intellectual property created by the Receiver, even if not original, is considered work for hire and belongs to VIDAR, conflicting with the implicit understanding that the agreement is related to original works of authorship.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nothing in this Agreement shall be cons trued as granting or conferring any rights, interests or intellectual property right s by assignment, license or otherwise, relating to any Confidential Information in or for any intellectual property, invention, discovery or improvement or derivative made, conceived or acquired prior or subsequent to the Effective Date. Except for the limited right to use the Confidential Information as set forth above, all intellectual property, patent, copyright, trademark, trade secret and a ll other rights and interests in the Confidential Information and the products, inventions, ideas, works, creation, symbols, data, reports, opinions and all other concepts and materials developed, invented, reduced to practice, authored or created by Receiver (including portions and derivatives thereof) in any medi um arising from or relating to this Agreement (“ Intellectual Property ”) shall be the sole and exclusive property of VIDAR.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the scope of 'works made for hire' by removing the limitation to 'original works of authorship.' This contradicts the contradicted text, which defines 'Intellectual Property' as arising 'from or relating to this Agreement' and developed by Receiver, implicitly related to original works. By eliminating the 'original works of authorship' clause, the changed text implies that *any* intellectual property, even if not original and not necessarily related to the agreement, is considered work for hire, thus belonging to VIDAR, conflicting with the contradicted section. This is an Omissions - In Text Contradiction. This creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Receiver shall promptly disclose a ll Intellectual Property to VIDAR and shall make and maintain adequate and current written records of all Intellectual Property. Receiver agrees to take all actions, execute all instruments and fully cooperate with VIDAR, its successors, assigns or nominees in which, in the opinion of VIDAR, are expedient or necessa ry at any time to file, prosecute, register, evidence, secure, maintain or lit igate all such VIDAR rights and interests in the Intellectual Property throughout the wo rld, including all commercial, legal or administrative proceedings involving the same. All expenses of applying for and obtaining such rights and interests sha ll be borne by VIDAR. VIDAR shall reimburse Receiver for pre-approved expenses paid in connection with such cooperation.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiver shall disclose all Intellectual Property to VIDAR. Receiver agrees to cooperate with VIDAR in the creation of Intellectual Property. All expenses of applying for and obtaining such rights and interests sha ll be borne by VIDAR.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the requirements for the Receiver to 'make and maintain adequate and current written records of all Intellectual Property' and to 'take all actions' necessary to secure VIDAR's rights. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 5(a), which asserts that VIDAR has sole ownership of Intellectual Property. Without the receiver being required to maintain records, VIDAR's ability to enforce that ownership becomes uncertain and potentially impossible to verify what property is intellectual property of VIDAR.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nothing in this Agreement shall be cons trued as granting or conferring any rights, interests or intellectual property right s by assignment, license or otherwise, relating to any Confidential Information in or for any intellectual property, invention, discovery or improvement or derivative made, conceived or acquired prior or subsequent to the Effective Date. Except for the limited right to use the Confidential Information as set forth above, all intellectual property, patent, copyright, trademark, trade secret and a ll other rights and interests in the Confidential Information and the products, inventions, ideas, works, creation, symbols, data, reports, opinions and all other concepts and materials developed, invented, reduced to practice, authored or created by Receiver (including portions and derivatives thereof) in any medi um arising from or relating to this Agreement (“ Intellectual Property ”) shall be the sole and exclusive property of VIDAR.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the Receiver's obligation to maintain records of Intellectual Property. The contradicted text explicitly states that the Intellectual Property shall be the sole and exclusive property of VIDAR. By removing the Receiver's obligation to maintain records, VIDAR's ability to prove and enforce its sole ownership is significantly weakened, creating a direct contradiction. The absence of required records directly undermines VIDAR's ability to enforce its ownership, leading to uncertainty and potential legal challenges. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition, where essential language is removed, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: VIDAR may terminate this Agreement at any time for any reason.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'at any time for any reason', the contract introduces ambiguity. The initial clause implies a level of freedom to terminate that is now diminished. Section 6, which states 'Upon the request of VIDAR or the termination or expiration of this Confidential Agreement, Re ceiver shall promptly return to VIDAR all copies of the Confidential Information', is now in conflict because if the termination isn't for 'any reason', then other reasons might exclude that, or make the termination invalid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the request of VIDAR or the termination or expiration of this Confidential Agreement, Re ceiver shall promptly return to VIDAR all copies of the Confidential Information and obtained by Receiver.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed VIDAR to terminate the agreement 'at any time for any reason'. The changed text removes the 'at any time for any reason' clause, restricting VIDAR's termination rights. The contradicted text, stating that 'Upon the request of VIDAR or the termination... Receiver shall promptly return...', relies on the assumption that VIDAR has the right to terminate. By removing 'at any time for any reason', the contract introduces ambiguity regarding whether a specific termination reason falls within the scope of valid reasons. This directly impacts the receiver's obligation to return the Confidential Information because it's unclear when the termination clause applies, and hence creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Receiver shall reimburse VIDAR for all costs and expenses, including attorney’s fees, incurr ed by VIDAR in enforcing the obligations of Receiver.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiver may be required to reimburse VIDAR for costs incurred by VIDAR in enforcing the obligations of Receiver.
perturbation.explanation: The original language creates a clear obligation for the receiver to reimburse VIDAR for enforcement costs, including attorney's fees. The change to 'may be required' introduces ambiguity and weakens VIDAR's position. This directly contradicts Section 8's assertion that VIDAR 'can not be made whole by monetary damages' if the agreement is breached. Because the receiver doesn't necessarily have to reimburse VIDAR, this creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiver acknowledges and agrees that: (i) the Confidential Information is proprietary to and valuable information of VIDAR; (ii) VIDAR derives economic value from the Confidential Information not being generally known to other persons who can obtain economic value from its discl osure or use; (iii) any disclosure or unauthorized use of the Confidential Information could cause irreparable harm and loss to VIDAR; and (iv) if this Agr eement is breached, VIDAR can not be made whole by monetary damages. Accordingly, VIDAR, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled by law or in equity, shall be entitled to an injunction to prevent further breaches of this Agreem ent and to an order compelling specific performance of this Agreement. Receiver may be required to reimburse VIDAR for costs incurred by VIDAR in enforcing the obligations of Receiver.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates reimbursement, while the changed text makes it discretionary ('may be required'). This weakens VIDAR's position and contradicts the assertion in the contradicted text (which now contains the changed text) that VIDAR 'can not be made whole by monetary damages' if the agreement is breached because it introduces uncertainty about whether VIDAR will be reimbursed for enforcement costs. This is a direct conflict causing uncertainty and an in-text contradiction, and aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.
